title: Dabarīm Kapturīm
subtitle: Cretan Commandments
source: Shirat Ha Kotharot — Tom di Mino

precepts.minoan: Bi-yad puluḥ ʿidān ḥaddu — ʿal qašqash, ʿal ʾaban, ʿal qanah.
precepts.linear_a: PI-JA-DU PU-RU-KU I-DA-NU KA-DU — A-RA QA-SA-QA-SA — A-RA A-PA-NU — A-RA QA-NA
precepts.phoenician: 𐤁𐤉𐤃 𐤐𐤋𐤇 𐤏𐤃𐤍 𐤇𐤃 — 𐤏𐤋 𐤒𐤔𐤒𐤔 𐤏𐤋 𐤀𐤁𐤍 𐤏𐤋 𐤒𐤍
precepts.english: By hand, revere the sharp moment — upon scale, upon stone, upon reed.
precepts.original: Palpably, revere the moment's prickle, on scale, or stone, or stem.

precepts.minoan: Qarāʾtu ʿamm-ī: yilbashū ʿōr-ī, hin ʾaḥzē ʾaḥarīt u qadmōnīt — ʿidanāt ḥurāṣ lipnē kull u ʾaḥarē kull.
precepts.linear_a: QA-RA-TU A-MI — JA-RI-PA-SU O-RI — A-KA-ZE A-KA-RI-TI U QA-DU-MO-NI-TI — I-DA-NA-TI KU-RA-ṢU RI-PU-NE-NA U A-KA-RE-NA KI-RO
precepts.phoenician: 𐤒𐤓𐤀𐤕 𐤏𐤌𐤉 𐤉𐤋𐤁𐤔 𐤏𐤓𐤉 𐤄𐤍 𐤀𐤇𐤆 𐤀𐤇𐤓𐤕 𐤅 𐤒𐤃𐤌𐤍𐤕 𐤏𐤃𐤍𐤕 𐤇𐤓𐤑 𐤋𐤐𐤍𐤍 𐤅 𐤀𐤇𐤓𐤍 𐤊𐤋
precepts.english: I called out to my people: they may wear my hide, if I may divine the future and the ancient-past — ages of gold before all and after all.
precepts.original: I have told my kin, they may wear my skin, if I may peer in the future and the past—the Golden Ages before and after us all.

words.source: Ug. kl, Heb. kol, Lin.A KI-RO

precepts.minoan: Qayinat-nā ashar bi-Shamē u bi-ʾArṣ, tanashshipu dabarāt-hā bi-ʾuznāt ʾamunōt.
precepts.linear_a: QA-JA-NI-TA-NA A-SA-RA PI-SA-ME U PI-A-RU-ṢU — TA-NA-SI-PU DA-PA-RA-TA PI-U-ZU-NA-TI A-MU-NO-TI
precepts.phoenician: 𐤒𐤉𐤍𐤕𐤍 𐤀𐤔𐤓 𐤁𐤔𐤌 𐤅 𐤁𐤀𐤓𐤑 𐤕𐤍𐤔𐤐 𐤃𐤁𐤓𐤕𐤄 𐤁𐤀𐤆𐤍𐤕 𐤀𐤌𐤍𐤕
precepts.english: Our Creatrix, who is in the Heavens and the Earth, breathes forth her words through the ears of the faithful.
precepts.original: Our creatrix, who art in the Heavens and the Earth, whispers her words through the ears of the faithful.

precepts.minoan: Ani malāḥ — u ẓillu naẓar min ʿammīm, maqōmāt, dōrōt.
precepts.linear_a: A-NI MA-RA-KU — U ṢI-RU NA-ṢA-RU MI-NU A-MI-MU MA-QO-MA-TI DU-RO-TI
precepts.phoenician: 𐤀𐤍𐤉 𐤌𐤋𐤇 — 𐤅 𐤑𐤋 𐤍𐤑𐤓 𐤌𐤍 𐤏𐤌𐤌 𐤌𐤒𐤌𐤕 𐤃𐤓𐤕
precepts.english: I am a sailor — a shadow glimpsed from peoples, places, eras.
precepts.original: I am a sailor — the shadow glimpsed by other peoples, of other places, of other eras.

words.source: Ar. ẓill, Heb. ṣel (צ < PS *ẓ), Akk. ṣillu

precepts.minoan: Nōśiʾ ṣōripī lahabu ʾishtu kīma mayim, nāshipu ʿabur barqu — naʿlē maʿaṭ ʿillōy, u naḥpur ʿamīq, yōman kull u kull.
precepts.linear_a: NO-SI ṢO-RI-PI RA-HA-PU I-SI-TU KI-MA MA-JI-MU — NA-SI-PU A-PU-RU PA-RU-QU — NA-A-RE MA-A-TU I-RO-JA U NA-KA-PU-RU A-MI-QU JO-MA-NU KI-RO U KI-RO
precepts.phoenician: 𐤍𐤔𐤀 𐤑𐤓𐤐𐤉 𐤋𐤄𐤁 𐤀𐤔𐤕 𐤊𐤌 𐤌𐤉𐤌 𐤍𐤔𐤐 𐤏𐤁𐤓 𐤁𐤓𐤒 𐤍𐤏𐤋 𐤌𐤏𐤈 𐤏𐤋𐤉 𐤅 𐤍𐤇𐤐𐤓 𐤏𐤌𐤒 𐤉𐤌𐤍 𐤊𐤋 𐤅 𐤊𐤋
precepts.english: Wielding a refiner's blade of fire like water, breathing through lightning — may we rise a little higher, and dig deeper, daily, all and all.
precepts.original: Wielding an alchemical foil, a fire that is like water, that breathes through skin like lightning, may we each rise a little higher, and dig a little deeper, each and every day.

words.source: Ug. brq, Heb. baraq, Akk. birqu
Ug. kl, Heb. kol, Lin.A KI-RO

precepts.minoan: Kull ʿōlām tashbītu, ka ḥarāshat u kiništu, lā-ʿōlām dōrī.
precepts.linear_a: KI-RO A-RA-MU TA-SA-PI-TU — KA KA-RA-SA-TA U KI-NI-SI-TU — RA-A-RA-MU DU-RI
precepts.phoenician: 𐤊𐤋 𐤏𐤋𐤌 𐤕𐤔𐤁𐤕 𐤊 𐤇𐤓𐤔𐤕 𐤅 𐤊𐤍𐤔𐤕 𐤋𐤏𐤋𐤌 𐤃𐤓𐤉
precepts.english: All eternity is a reverberation; like craft and civilization, forever cyclical.
precepts.original: All time is but an echo; like art and civilization, it is always cyclical.

words.source: Ug. kl, Heb. kol, Lin.A KI-RO

precepts.english: The priestess takes the bull by the horns.

words.source: Ug. ṯr, Heb. shōr (via ṯ > sh)
